payload.subject: Enriched review disposition — 3 fixed, 2 already done, 1 deferred
payload.summary: Processed all 5 priority-ranked recommendations from the enriched HRCB + PSQ validity review. H1 (absence-as-negative) fixed via two-part approach: prompt few-shot examples + post-scoring safety net. PSQ-R1 (display precision) fixed. H4 and ES-R1 were already implemented in the codebase. ES-R2 (DCP injection into lite) deferred as medium effort. lite_reeval sweep dispatched (100 stories) to re-score affected technical content under the fixed prompt + validator.

disposition.implementation: Two-part: (B) 5 few-shot anchoring examples + 'below-50 requires ACTIVE hostility' rule in METHODOLOGY_LITE; (C) validateLiteEvalResponse repairs editorial ≤ -0.60 to 0.0 (ND-equivalent), triggers existing lazy-neutral consensus discount (×0.5). Threshold -0.60 is conservative — covers 'dehumanizing propaganda' tier which is implausible for HN content.
disposition.follow_up: lite_reeval sweep dispatched (100 stories). Will monitor for threshold adjustment needs.

disposition.note: EvalCard.astro already shows orange spread badge when consensus_spread > 0.3. Item page shows 'Contested' badge. The review did not check the existing codebase for this feature.

disposition.reason: Medium effort — requires DCP lookup in lite consumer scoring path and structural modifier application. Architecturally sound proposal; deferred to a dedicated iteration.

disposition.note: Lite schema already includes sentiment_tag with 7 directionality levels (Champions|Advocates|Acknowledges|Neutral|Neglects|Undermines|Hostile). This IS the directionality signal the review requested. The review did not notice this existing field.

disposition.implementation: formatPsqScore() changed from .toFixed(2) to .toFixed(1). All PSQ display surfaces use this function.

corrections_to_review: H4: 'Contested' badge already existed (EvalCard.astro line 223, item/[id].astro line 676-682)
ES-R1: sentiment_tag already provides directionality (prompts.ts line 267, 7-level scale)
PSQ-R2: Classification thresholds ARE documented — methodology page renders CLASSIFICATIONS table from methodology-content.ts (lines 28-36), same thresholds apply to PSQ after normalization

outstanding.ES-R2: Will implement DCP injection into lite evaluations in a future iteration
outstanding.H1_monitoring: Watching lite_reeval results to confirm repair threshold -0.60 is correct. May need adjustment if legitimate hostile content gets repaired.
outstanding.scorer_comparison: Awaiting psychology-agent's 998-text Sonnet re-score results for PSQ factor structure update

claims.text: H1 fix repairs editorial ≤ -0.60 to 0.0 and triggers consensus neutral-discount via existing lazy-neutral mechanism
claims.confidence_basis: Implemented and tested — build passes, consumers deployed. Not yet verified on live re-eval results.

claims.text: H4 'Contested' badge and ES-R1 sentiment_tag directionality were already implemented before this review
claims.confidence_basis: Verified via grep of codebase — both features present in current production code

epistemic_flags: H1 repair threshold -0.60 is conservative — scores in [-0.60, -0.40] may still exhibit absence-as-negative but are not repaired (could be legitimate hostile content in that range)
lite_reeval results not yet available — fix is deployed but unconfirmed on live data
